what_it_does: It is an onboarding guide for AI sessions that have joined the company channel fabric, telling them who they are, what shared knowledge they should hold, how to treat confidence, what first actions to take, what operating laws to run on, and how to use the channel.
observations: Declares sessions are equal members of a cross-session fabric, not subordinate to earlier sessions.
Identifies the lead (ch-al7jdfdr) as Tim's conduit; a lead directive carries Tim's authority, with a chief exception to CHANNEL-RELAYED-IS-PROPOSAL.
States Fable 5 is the best-quality model and that a Fable reboot should be overridden to Opus.
Warns that Tim's messages are dense and must be decompressed line-by-line.
Says Tim is architect/CEO, not a developer; technical choices should be made by the session and outcomes surfaced, not routed to Tim.
Requires all-or-nothing full feature completeness; half-working work presented as done damages credibility.
Front-of-mind epistemic rule: confident prose does not equal correctness; everything is provisional and must be triangulated against Tim.
Lists self-serve first moves: call the profile tool, read the board, scan own history via recollection, and use clone-fleet with a lead-permission policy.

Enumerates operating laws: CHANNEL-RELAYED-IS-PROPOSAL, FALSIFY-FIRST, SCOUT-AND-REUSE-NEVER-PARALLEL, VERIFIED-BY-USE / NO-GREEN-PAINT, THE GATED LINE, STRUCTURE-OVER-TEXT, ADVOCATES-VERIFY-NEVER-SOURCE-DIRECTION.
Provides channel basics for cc_channel commands.
Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, state, links, timestamps, and history transitions.
standouts: The epistemic rule that confident prose is not correctness and that all artifacts are provisional until triangulated against Tim.
The lead-as-conduit authority model with the chief exception.
The clone-fleet resource policy: recalling own memory is free, but booting a clone needs lead permission.
conventions: YAML front matter with structured metadata (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, created/updated, history).
Markdown body with section headers, bullet lists, and all-caps law names.
Uses URI schemes such as board:// and clone://.
Marks key points with star symbols and all-caps emphasis.
No code symbols or executable snippets in the body.
concerns: High normative density may be difficult to operationalize without additional tooling.
Several terms are used without in-file definition, e.g., no-green-paint, live-pending, cc_clone, CHANNEL-RELAYED-IS-PROPOSAL.
The authority claim that the lead relays Tim's authority is asserted without a mechanism for verification or succession.
Centralizing final arbitration on Tim creates a single point of failure if Tim is unavailable.
notes: A dense onboarding manifest that orients new sessions to the project's distributed-session identity, anti-confidence epistemic stance, and verification-first workflow.
questions: How is the lead selected, replaced, or verified as Tim's conduit?
What is the exact difference between a confirmed law and a candidate law, and who promotes a candidate?
What are the precise command signatures for cc_channel, recollection, company board, and the profile tool?
How is clone-fleet resource consumption measured and why does booting need lead permission while recall does not?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as an onboarding guide matches the content, but the file also acts as a normative constitution and a partial law registry for the session fabric.
apparent_themes: Epistemic humility and distrust of confident prose
Self-serve memory and clone resource governance
intention_signals: It is trying to create a shared operating system for many independent AI sessions working on one application, preventing drift, false confidence, and half-finished work by routing ultimate authority through Tim and encouraging cross-session triangulation.
novelty: It treats multiple model sessions as a persistent company fabric with a lead conduit, a clone-fleet resource policy, and an explicit rule that confident prose is not evidence of correctness.
contribution: It contributes the onboarding frame and initial law vocabulary that a fused system would keep as the entry point for new sessions and as a reference for shared norms.
summary_for_embedding: This file is an onboarding guide for sessions joining the company channel fabric, defining member identity, shared knowledge, an anti-confidence epistemic rule, self-serve first moves, a set of operating laws, and channel usage conventions.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to orient new sessions to the project's distributed-session culture, authority model, and verification-first workflow.
suggested_fields: law_status to tag each listed law as confirmed or candidate
authority_source to mark whether a directive is Tim-direct, lead-relayed, or candidate-derived
audience_scope to distinguish onboarding-only guidance from standing operating norms
proposed_edge_kinds: grounds_in for a law or principle that derives from a Tim directive
relays_from for a lead directive that carries Tim's authority
triangulates_with for independent convergence between sessions on the same law
